template.name: rAthena Services Monitoring
template.description:
Comprehensive monitoring for rAthena game server infrastructure. Includes: TCP service checks (Login/Char/Map), host resources (CPU/memory/disk/network) via Agent2, MariaDB metrics (connections/QPS/slow queries/buffer pool), and backup status. Triggers with severity-based alerting and webhook notifications.
Importable via: Configuration → Templates → Import (Zabbix 7.0 LTS)
Requirements covered: 5.2, 5.3, 5.4, 5.5, 5.6, 5.7, 5.8, 5.9, 5.10

macro.description: Host where rAthena services are running
CPU utilization threshold for High severity alert (%)
Memory utilization threshold for High severity alert (%)
Free disk space threshold for Disaster severity alert (%)
Duration of TCP failure before Disaster alert
Slow queries per minute threshold for Warning alert
Webhook type: discord, telegram, or slack

item.name: rAthena Login Server: TCP port {$RATHENA.LOGIN.PORT} status
item.description: Checks TCP connectivity to Login Server port. Returns 1 if service is up, 0 if down.

item.name: rAthena Char Server: TCP port {$RATHENA.CHAR.PORT} status
item.description: Checks TCP connectivity to Char Server port. Returns 1 if service is up, 0 if down.

item.name: rAthena Map Server: TCP port {$RATHENA.MAP.PORT} status
item.description: Checks TCP connectivity to Map Server port. Returns 1 if service is up, 0 if down.

item.name: rAthena Login Server: TCP port {$RATHENA.LOGIN.PORT} response time
item.description: TCP response time for Login Server port in seconds.

item.name: rAthena Char Server: TCP port {$RATHENA.CHAR.PORT} response time
item.description: TCP response time for Char Server port in seconds.

item.name: rAthena Map Server: TCP port {$RATHENA.MAP.PORT} response time
item.description: TCP response time for Map Server port in seconds.

item.name: Host: CPU utilization
item.description: Total CPU utilization percentage via Zabbix Agent2.

item.name: Host: CPU idle time
item.description: CPU idle time percentage. Used for trigger calculation (100 - idle = utilization).

item.name: Host: CPU system time
item.description: CPU time spent in kernel space.

item.name: Host: CPU user time
item.description: CPU time spent in user space.

item.name: Host: CPU I/O wait time
item.description: CPU time waiting for I/O operations.

item.name: Host: Load average (1 min)
item.description: System load average over the last 1 minute.

item.name: Host: Load average (5 min)
item.description: System load average over the last 5 minutes.

item.name: Host: Memory utilization
item.description: Percentage of memory currently in use.

item.name: Host: Total memory
item.description: Total physical memory in bytes.

item.name: Host: Available memory
item.description: Available memory (free + cached + buffers) in bytes.

item.name: Host: Swap utilization
item.description: Percentage of swap space currently in use.

item.name: Host: Disk free space on / (percentage)
item.description: Percentage of free disk space on root filesystem.

item.name: Host: Disk total space on /
item.description: Total disk space on root filesystem in bytes.

item.name: Host: Disk used space on /
item.description: Used disk space on root filesystem in bytes.

item.name: Host: Disk read rate
item.description: Disk read operations per second.

item.name: Host: Disk write rate
item.description: Disk write operations per second.

item.name: Host: Network incoming traffic (eth0)
item.description: Incoming network traffic on primary interface in bits per second.

item.name: Host: Network outgoing traffic (eth0)
item.description: Outgoing network traffic on primary interface in bits per second.

item.name: Host: Network incoming errors (eth0)
item.description: Number of incoming network errors per second.

item.name: Host: Network outgoing errors (eth0)
item.description: Number of outgoing network errors per second.

item.name: MariaDB: Active connections (Threads_connected)
item.description: Number of currently active (connected) client threads in MariaDB.

item.name: MariaDB: Queries per second
item.description: Number of SQL queries executed per second (QPS).

item.name: MariaDB: Slow queries per minute
item.description: Number of slow queries per minute (queries exceeding long_query_time).

item.name: MariaDB: InnoDB buffer pool utilization
item.description: Percentage of InnoDB buffer pool pages currently in use.

item.name: MariaDB: InnoDB buffer pool size
item.description: Total InnoDB buffer pool size in bytes.

item.name: rAthena: Backup last status
item.description: Last backup exit code. 0 = success, non-zero = failure. File written by backup.sh after each execution.

item.name: rAthena: Backup last timestamp
item.description: Timestamp of the last backup status file modification (indicates when the last backup ran).

trigger.name: rAthena Login Server is DOWN (port {$RATHENA.LOGIN.PORT} unreachable for {$TCP.FAIL.DURATION})
trigger.description: Login Server has not responded on TCP port {$RATHENA.LOGIN.PORT} for {$TCP.FAIL.DURATION}. Players cannot authenticate or connect to the game.

trigger.name: rAthena Char Server is DOWN (port {$RATHENA.CHAR.PORT} unreachable for {$TCP.FAIL.DURATION})
trigger.description: Char Server has not responded on TCP port {$RATHENA.CHAR.PORT} for {$TCP.FAIL.DURATION}. Players cannot select characters or enter the game.

trigger.name: rAthena Map Server is DOWN (port {$RATHENA.MAP.PORT} unreachable for {$TCP.FAIL.DURATION})
trigger.description: Map Server has not responded on TCP port {$RATHENA.MAP.PORT} for {$TCP.FAIL.DURATION}. Players are disconnected from the game world.

trigger.name: Disk space critically low (free <{$DISK.THRESHOLD.DISASTER}%)
trigger.description: Free disk space has dropped below {$DISK.THRESHOLD.DISASTER}%. Immediate action required — database writes and backups may fail.

trigger.name: CPU utilization is too high (>{$CPU.THRESHOLD.HIGH}% for 5 min)
trigger.description: CPU utilization has exceeded {$CPU.THRESHOLD.HIGH}% for the last 5 minutes. Guard condition: idle must be >0% to avoid false alerts from invalid 0% readings. Investigate resource-heavy processes or consider scaling.

trigger.name: Memory utilization is too high (>{$MEMORY.THRESHOLD.HIGH}% for 3 min)
trigger.description: Memory utilization has exceeded {$MEMORY.THRESHOLD.HIGH}% for the last 3 minutes. Risk of OOM killer activation. Consider adding swap or increasing memory allocation.

trigger.name: rAthena backup failed (exit code <> 0)
trigger.description: The last database backup failed with a non-zero exit code. Check backup logs in /backups/ for details. Manual intervention may be required to restore backup functionality.

trigger.name: MariaDB: Slow queries exceed {$SLOW.QUERIES.THRESHOLD}/min
trigger.description: MariaDB is executing more than {$SLOW.QUERIES.THRESHOLD} slow queries per minute (queries exceeding long_query_time=2s). Review slow query log at /var/lib/mysql/slow-query.log and consider query optimization or index additions.

media_type.name: rAthena Alert Webhook
media_type.description:
Sends alert notifications to Discord, Telegram, or Slack via webhook.
Configuration: 1. Set {$ALERT.WEBHOOK.URL} macro to your webhook endpoint URL 2. Set {$ALERT.WEBHOOK.TYPE} macro to: discord, telegram, or slack 3. For Telegram, configure the chat_id in webhook parameters
Severity color mapping: - Disaster: Red (16711680) - High: Orange (16744448) - Warning: Yellow (16776960)
